manifest.title: PEFE Pulse-Gate S11 Explorer — 22 July 2026
manifest.description: Interactive three-level pulse-gate S11 and temperature explorer for the usable acquisitions recorded on 22 July 2026.

cards.description: The notebook-selected 1 s bin. The same window is bounded by the two vertical guides in Level 1.
The notebook-selected pulse within that second. The Level 2 reference line marks this pulse.
Temperature interpolated at the selected pulse timestamp.
Retained HIGH-gate samples passed to Level 3 after the notebook quality and edge filters.
End minus start temperature across the aligned Level 1 window.
Median of the one-point-per-second S11 magnitude series.
Last minus first unwrapped Level 1 S11 phase.

charts.title: Level 1 — Global temperature
charts.subtitle: The two vertical guides bound the selected 1 s window; temperature uses the same time basis as Level 1 S11.
charts.question: How does temperature vary across time from selected-second centre?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 1 — Global pulse-gate S11 magnitude (1 point/s)
charts.subtitle: Notebook-style global view. The vertical guides bound the selected second used by Level 2.
charts.question: How does s11 magnitude vary across time from selected-second centre?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 1 — Global pulse-gate S11 phase (1 point/s)
charts.subtitle: Circularly aggregated and unwrapped. The vertical guides bound the selected second.
charts.question: How does s11 phase vary across time from selected-second centre?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 2 — Temperature inside selected 1 s
charts.subtitle: The vertical reference marks the selected 1 ms pulse that feeds Level 3.
charts.question: How does temperature vary across time from selected pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 2 — Pulse-gate S11 magnitude inside selected 1 s
charts.subtitle: One RF HIGH pulse-gate estimate per valid 1 ms pulse; the reference line is the selected pulse.
charts.question: How does s11 magnitude vary across time from selected pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 2 — Pulse-gate phase inside selected 1 s
charts.subtitle: Unwrapped phase; the reference line is the selected pulse passed to Level 3.
charts.question: How does s11 phase vary across time from selected pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 3 — Selected-pulse temperature
charts.subtitle: Temperature at the selected pulse, repeated over its microsecond-scale HIGH gate for alignment.
charts.question: How does temperature vary across time inside selected 1 ms pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 3 — Selected 1 ms pulse S11 magnitude
charts.subtitle: Only retained RF HIGH samples after notebook edge trimming are plotted.
charts.question: How does s11 magnitude vary across time inside selected 1 ms pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

charts.title: Level 3 — Selected 1 ms pulse phase (HIGH gate only)
charts.subtitle: Only retained RF HIGH samples after notebook edge trimming are plotted.
charts.question: How does s11 phase vary across time inside selected 1 ms pulse?
charts.rationale: A line chart preserves the ordered acquisition axis and makes local changes visible.

tables.title: Selected experiment details
tables.subtitle: Exact acquisition and drill-down coverage for the current selection.

query.description: Run-level metadata and derived quality/coverage summary for the usable acquisitions.
filters: Run date is 22 July 2026.
RF OFF and all out-of-pulse samples are excluded.
Pulse gates require coherence >= 0.90, sufficient master power, and gate width >= 4 samples.
metric_definitions: S11 magnitude is 20*log10(abs(mean(slave*conj(master))/mean(abs(master)^2))).
S11 phase uses circular aggregation and time-continuous unwrapping.
Temperature is linearly interpolated from runmeta temperature samples onto each S11 timestamp.

query.description: One pulse-gate S11 aggregate per second, with temperature interpolated to the same timestamps.
filters: Run date is 22 July 2026.
RF OFF and all out-of-pulse samples are excluded.
Pulse gates require coherence >= 0.90, sufficient master power, and gate width >= 4 samples.
metric_definitions: S11 magnitude is 20*log10(abs(mean(slave*conj(master))/mean(abs(master)^2))).
S11 phase uses circular aggregation and time-continuous unwrapping.
Temperature is linearly interpolated from runmeta temperature samples onto each S11 timestamp.

sources.label: Level 2 selected-second pulse-gate S11 and temperature

query.description: One S11 estimate per valid 1 ms pulse inside the selected second, with temperature interpolated per pulse.
filters: Run date is 22 July 2026.
RF OFF and all out-of-pulse samples are excluded.
Pulse gates require coherence >= 0.90, sufficient master power, and gate width >= 4 samples.
metric_definitions: S11 magnitude is 20*log10(abs(mean(slave*conj(master))/mean(abs(master)^2))).
S11 phase uses circular aggregation and time-continuous unwrapping.
Temperature is linearly interpolated from runmeta temperature samples onto each S11 timestamp.

sources.label: Level 3 selected-pulse HIGH-gate samples and temperature

query.description: Samplewise S11 within the edge-trimmed RF HIGH gate of the selected 1 ms pulse; temperature is constant over this microsecond-scale view.
filters: Run date is 22 July 2026.
RF OFF and all out-of-pulse samples are excluded.
Pulse gates require coherence >= 0.90, sufficient master power, and gate width >= 4 samples.
metric_definitions: S11 magnitude is 20*log10(abs(mean(slave*conj(master))/mean(abs(master)^2))).
S11 phase uses circular aggregation and time-continuous unwrapping.
Temperature is linearly interpolated from runmeta temperature samples onto each S11 timestamp.

blocks.body:
Select an experiment with the **Experiment** control above. The selection panel then shows the exact notebook-selected second and pulse. Every temperature trace and S11 chart updates together. The three-level view begins immediately after the selection panel.

**Coverage:** 22 usable runs from 22 July 2026 are included. 1 run is excluded because no valid pulse-gate S11 remained after the notebook quality filters.

## Current notebook selection

The path is **selected second → selected pulse → retained HIGH-gate samples**. These values change with the experiment filter and define the three plotted levels below.

## Three-level pulse-gate view

Each level places temperature immediately above the matching S11 magnitude and phase charts. In Level 1, two vertical guides bound the selected 1 s window. In Level 2, the vertical reference marks the selected pulse. Level 3 contains only the retained HIGH-gate samples from that pulse.

## Level 1 — global run with selected-second window

One RF HIGH pulse-gate aggregate per second, aligned with temperature. The x-axis is centred on the selected second so the two guides always show its exact 1 s boundary.

## Level 2 — selected 1 second with selected-pulse marker

One RF HIGH pulse-gate estimate per valid 1 ms pulse, aligned with temperature. Zero on the x-axis is the pulse selected for Level 3.

## Level 3 — selected 1 ms pulse

Samplewise S11 inside the edge-trimmed RF HIGH gate of the selected pulse; temperature is shown on the same microsecond axis.

## Method and downloads

This page implements the computation used by `TMTT_v1_9_121347_S11_three_level_pulse_only.ipynb` without publishing the notebook itself. RF OFF, out-of-pulse samples, low-power gates, low-coherence gates, and insufficient-width gates are excluded. The page is a published snapshot, not a live instrument connection.

[Download all processed CSV data](https://tianqr18-richrd.github.io/TianQR18.github.io/downloads/pefe_s11_2026-07-22_processed_data.zip)
